Package org.mitre.synthea.world.geography

Class Demographics

java.lang.Object
org.mitre.synthea.world.geography.Demographics
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<Demographics>
public class Demographics extends Object implements Comparable<Demographics>, Serializable
Demographics class holds the information from the towns.json and associated county config files. This data is used to build up a synthetic population matching these real-world statistics. A single instance of Demographics represents a single city or town. The Ages, Gender, Race, Income, and Education properties are maps of frequency information. TODO: add ways to better wrap these maps so they are more accessible and useful. TODO: merge this with Location somehow. they probably don't need to be separate classes
See Also:

  • Method Details

    • pickAge

      public int pickAge(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Pick an age based on the population distribution for the city.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the age in years

    • pickGender

      public String pickGender(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Pick a gender based on the population distribution for the city.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the gender

    • pickRace

      public String pickRace(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Pick a race based on the population distribution for the city. Uses the US Census definition for race.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the race

    • pickEthnicity

      public String pickEthnicity(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Pick an ethnicity based on the population distribution for the city. Uses the US Census definition for ethnicity.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      "hispanic" or "nonhispanic"

    • languageFromRaceAndEthnicity

      public String languageFromRaceAndEthnicity(String race, String ethnicity, RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Selects a language based on race and ethnicity. For those of Hispanic ethnicity, language statistics are pulled from the national distribution of spoken languages. For non-Hispanic, national distributions by race are used.
      Parameters:
      race - US Census race
      ethnicity - "hispanic" or "nonhispanic"
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the language spoken

    • pickIncome

      public int pickIncome(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Pick an income level based on the population distribution for the city.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the income level

    • incomeLevel

      public double incomeLevel(int income)
      Simple linear formula just maps federal poverty level to 0.0 and 75,000 to 1.0. The 75,000 figure was chosen based on https://www.princeton.edu/~deaton/downloads/deaton_kahneman_high_income_improves_evaluation_August2010.pdf
      Parameters:
      income - Annual income.
      Returns:
      a value between 0.0 and 1.0 representing the income level.

    • povertyRatio

      public double povertyRatio(int income)
      Return the poverty ratio.
      Parameters:
      income - Annual income.
      Returns:
      poverty ratio.

    • pickEducation

      public String pickEducation(RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Return a random education level based on statistics.
      Parameters:
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      the randomly selected education level

    • educationLevel

      public double educationLevel(String level, RandomNumberGenerator random)
      Return a random number between the configured bounds for a specified education level.
      Parameters:
      level - the education level, one of "less_than_hs", "hs_degree", "some_college", "bs_degree"
      random - the random number generator to use
      Returns:
      a random number between the configured bounds representing the education level

    • socioeconomicScore

      public double socioeconomicScore(double income, double education, double occupation)
      Calculate the socio-economic score for the supplied parameters.
      Parameters:
      income - the income level
      education - the education level
      occupation - the occupation level
      Returns:
      the socio-economic score

    • socioeconomicCategory

      public String socioeconomicCategory(double score)
      Return a high/middle/low socio economic category based on the supplied score and the configured stratifier values.
      Parameters:
      score - the socio-economic score
      Returns:
      the socio-economic category, one of "High", "Middle", or "Low"

    • load

      public static com.google.common.collect.Table<String,String,Demographics> load(String state) throws IOException
      Get a Table of (State, CityId, Demographics), with the given restrictions on state and city.
      Parameters:
      state - The state that is desired. Other states will be excluded from the results.
      Returns:
      Table of (State, CityId, Demographics)
      Throws:
      IOException - if any exception occurs in reading the demographics file

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(Demographics o)
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<Demographics>
